e_visa.notes: E-visa mandatory for US nationals as of April 6, 2026. Tourist (TR-V) and Business (TR-N) visa categories available. Apply at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Visa delivered via email. Fee reported at USD 180 (unconfirmed from official Venezuelan source - see gaps). Processing approximately 15 days. Visa valid 1 year, multiple entry.

As of April 2026, Venezuela operates a mandatory electronic visa (e-visa) system for all nationalities that require a visa to enter Venezuela. Applications submitted online at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Visas delivered electronically by email. Tourist (TR-V) and Business (TR-N) visa categories available. Processing time approximately 15 days (reduced from approximately 6 weeks). Visa validity 1 year, multiple entry. Fee amount unconfirmed from official Venezuelan sources; aggregators report USD 180. MPPRE published a user manual in February 2026 confirming the system is operational for North America tourist visa applications.

visa_policy.notes: The official MPPRE list at detalle_turismo/2 specifies 71 nationalities with visa-free tourism entry by air. All 71 nationalities receive a uniform 90-day stay allowance. Nationalities not on the visa-free list must apply for a visa. Since April 2026, Venezuela operates a mandatory electronic visa (e-visa) system via cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve for all visa-required nationalities, with tourist (TR-V) and business (TR-N) categories. The visa-free list does NOT include the USA, Canada, China, India, Kuwait, Serbia, or most African/Middle Eastern/Asian nations except those specifically listed. A MPPRE user manual dated February 2026 confirms the Cancillería Digital system is operational for tourist visa applications, including a specific North America guide.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid diplomatic and service passports of Serbia are exempt from visa requirements to enter, exit, transit, and remain in Venezuela for up to 90 days. Agreement signed in El Salvador on 6 June 2011; published in Venezuela's Gaceta Oficial No. 39.936 on 24 May 2012. Stay beyond 90 days requires prior authorisation. Holders must cross only through international entry points.

Holders of diplomatic, special (official), and service passports of Oman are exempt from visa requirements pursuant to the visa suppression agreement ('acuerdo de supresión de Visas para pasaportes diplomáticos, especiales y de servicios') signed at the 79th UN General Assembly session in New York on 23 September 2024. Stay limit and other conditions not specified in the MPPRE announcement; full treaty text not yet confirmed in Gaceta Oficial as of validation date.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Kyrgyzstan are exempt from visa requirements pursuant to the visa suppression agreement ('acuerdo de supresión de visas para titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos y de servicio') signed at the 79th UN General Assembly session in New York on 27 September 2024 - the first such agreement between Venezuela and Kyrgyzstan. Stay limit and other conditions not specified in the MPPRE announcement; full treaty text not yet confirmed in Gaceta Oficial as of validation date.

cbi.notes: Venezuela does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. No official CBI program found on any Venezuelan government website. Citizenship can only be obtained after 10 years of continuous lawful residency (or 5 years if married to a Venezuelan citizen), as per the standard naturalization path.

programs.name: Visa de Transeúnte Inversionista (TR-I)
programs.notes: Investor transient visa (TR-I) for foreign nationals who can demonstrate a materialized productive investment in Venezuela. Applicant must hold at least 20% ownership in a registered Venezuelan company or equivalent capital contribution. Visa granted for 3 years (multiple entry, no stay limit), extendable up to 2 additional years. After this, can apply for Resident status (5-year validity). Path to citizenship after 10 years continuous residency (5 years if married to Venezuelan national). No minimum investment amount is specified on the official consular pages. Applications must be submitted at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate.

outbound_official.note: no official Venezuela government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

validation_notes: Re-validated June 2026 against live official MPPRE page (mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2).
CORRECTED: e-visa tourist category code changed from 'T' to 'TR-V' (correct official Venezuelan category designation); business remains 'TR-N'.

CORRECTED: Removed earlier claim that the e-visa was 'no fee as of April 15, 2026'. Multiple 2026 sources report a fee of USD 180; however, no official Venezuelan government page confirmed fee amounts during this or prior research - noted as unconfirmed gap.

CORRECTED: eligible_nationalities in conditional_access entries changed from string 'any' to specific ISO3 arrays (SRB, OMN, KGZ) since each agreement is bilateral between Venezuela and one specific country, not open to all nationalities.
CONFIRMED: Visa-free list remains exactly 71 countries as listed on mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2 - re-fetched and matched entry-by-entry.
CONFIRMED: Serbia is NOT on the ordinary-passport visa-free list; its diplomatic/service waiver in conditional_access is correct.
CONFIRMED: Kuwait is NOT on the ordinary-passport visa-free list.
CONFIRMED: All 71 visa-free nationalities receive a uniform 90-day stay; no country-specific shorter limits appear on the official MPPRE page.
CONFIRMED: Saint Lucia ('Santa Lucía') is on the visa-free list - was already in record.
CONFIRMED: Oman (OMN) appears both in visa_free (ordinary passport, 90 days) AND in conditional_access (diplomatic/official/service passports) - these are separate and non-conflicting.
No new visa-free countries added to the official MPPRE list since last validation.

Re-validated 2026-06-27: Full 71-country visa-free list re-fetched and confirmed unchanged against live mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2. MPPRE also confirmed 13 visa categories via detalles_servicio/1, all matching existing visa_types entries. Cancillería Digital e-visa system confirmed operational via mppre.gob.ve/cancilleria-digital; a user manual dated February 2026 for tourist visas (North America) is published at MPPRE - confirming the e-visa system is active and used for North American applicants. Oman (mppre.gob.ve/publicacion/2951-0) and Kyrgyzstan (mppre.gob.ve/publicacion/3019) conditional_access agreements re-confirmed: diplomatic/service/official passport types only, no stay limit stated, no ratification date given. SAIME info.saime.gob.ve still returning ECONNREFUSED - cannot verify SAIME-side data.
MINOR UPDATE: Added mppre.gob.ve/cancilleria-digital as an additional source_url in e-visa entry and source_urls array, citing February 2026 user manual evidence of operational e-visa system.

Re-validated 2026-06-29: Official MPPRE visa-free list (mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2) re-fetched live — confirmed identical 71 countries, no additions or removals. Cancillería Digital page (mppre.gob.ve/cancilleria-digital) fetched: lists a 'Manual de Registro de Visa Pro 90' updated April 14, 2026 — this appears to be a new e-visa sub-category introduced in 2026. Eligibility, fee, max stay, and exact nationalities for Pro 90 could not be confirmed from fetched page content; added to gaps. Oman agreement (mppre.gob.ve/publicacion/2951-0) re-confirmed: signed September 23, 2024, diplomatic/special/service passports only; ratification status still not stated. SAIME info.saime.gob.ve still ECONNREFUSED. No changes made to visa_free, visa_on_arrival, e_visa, or eta lists — all confirmed unchanged.
gaps: The official MPPRE page (detalle_turismo/2) does not explicitly state the 90-day maximum stay for visa-free visitors; the 90-day figure is derived from uniform immigration practice and embassies.net confirms 90 days, but the SAIME notice at info.saime.gob.ve/noticia/209 was inaccessible (ECONNREFUSED) during this validation.

The e-visa fee is disputed: non-official aggregators and immigration law firms report USD 180; an earlier aggregator reported no fee as of April 15, 2026. No official Venezuelan government page confirmed fee amounts. Applicants should verify at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve.

The February 2026 MPPRE user manual PDF (mppre.gob.ve/gestor2/archivos/cancilleria_digital/Manual-para-el-registro-del-tramite-visa-de-turista-america-del-norte.pdf) returned HTTP 500 during this validation - could not read its full contents to confirm fee amounts or stay limits.
The investor visa TR-I has no minimum investment amount specified on any official consular page accessed.
SAIME info.saime.gob.ve returned DNS/connection failures preventing direct verification of stay limits, e-visa types, and other programs from SAIME pages.

No official data found confirming Venezuela's active participation in Mercosur or Andean Community free-movement frameworks - Venezuela was suspended from Mercosur in 2016 and withdrew from the Andean Community in 2006.
No digital nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled worker visa program found on any official Venezuelan government source.
The complete list of nationalities eligible only for e-visa (vs visa-free) has not been enumerated on any official page accessed.
Cuba, Panama, Honduras, Guatemala, Dominican Republic, Haiti, El Salvador are not on the visa-free list; their e-visa eligibility was not confirmed from official sources.
No official Venezuelan government source found confirming entry benefits for holders of third-country visas (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, etc.) or residence permits.

Approximately 47 additional countries cited in Wikipedia as having diplomatic/service/official passport waivers could not be verified from official Venezuelan government sources (Gaceta Oficial) during this validation. Only Serbia (Gaceta Oficial 2012), Oman (MPPRE 2024), and Kyrgyzstan (MPPRE 2024) were confirmed.
The Oman and Kyrgyzstan conditional_access agreements (signed September 2024) may not yet be formally in force - MPPRE announcements confirm signing but not ratification/entry-into-force dates.
Wikipedia notes the Venezuela-Argentina diplomatic passport waiver has been suspended; this could not be verified from an official Venezuelan government source.

NEW (2026-06-29): Cancillería Digital page lists a 'Manual de Registro de Visa Pro 90' dated April 14, 2026. This indicates a 'Pro 90' e-visa sub-category exists as of April 2026, but eligibility nationalities, fee, maximum stay duration, and whether it overlaps with or replaces TR-V tourist e-visa could not be determined from the fetched page. Full details must be verified at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve or through an MPPRE manual PDF.

visa_types.name: Visa de Turista (T)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Venezuela for recreation, leisure, health purposes, or other non-remunerated activities. Applies to nationalities not on the visa-free list.
visa_types.notes: Requires valid passport (6+ months), proof of residency, 2 photos, bank statement, employment letter, round-trip ticket, medical certificate, hotel reservation or invitation, and consular fee receipt. Fee amount must be confirmed at the relevant Venezuelan diplomatic or consular office. As of April 2026, Venezuela also offers an electronic visa option (TR-V tourist category) via cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Turista (TR-V) - Electronic
visa_types.purpose: Electronic tourist visa for foreign nationals wishing to enter Venezuela for tourism; mandatory e-visa system for all nationalities requiring a visa to enter Venezuela.
visa_types.notes: Introduced as part of Venezuela's mandatory electronic visa system as of April 2026. Applied online at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Visa delivered electronically via email. Fee of USD 180 is reported by multiple aggregators but has NOT been confirmed from an official Venezuelan government page - verify at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Processing approximately 10-15 days. A user manual (febrero 2026) for this category is published by MPPRE at mppre.gob.ve/cancilleria-digital.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Negocios (TR-N)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Venezuela to conduct business activities, attend meetings, negotiations, or commercial activities without taking up employment.
visa_types.notes: Available via Venezuela's mandatory electronic visa system (cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve) as of April 2026. Visa delivered electronically via email. Fee of USD 180 reported by aggregators but not confirmed from official Venezuelan government page - verify at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Also available through Venezuelan embassies and consulates for non-e-visa applications. A user manual for this category is published by MPPRE at mppre.gob.ve/cancilleria-digital.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Laboral (TR-L)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have been offered employment in Venezuela and wish to enter the country to work for a Venezuelan employer.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements, fees, and validity periods not published on official online sources; applicants must contact the nearest Venezuelan diplomatic mission for current requirements.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Estudiante (TR-E)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have been accepted to study at a recognized Venezuelan educational institution.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Requires proof of enrollment at a Venezuelan educational institution. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Inversionista (TR-I)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who make a productive investment in Venezuela, requiring at least 20% ownership in a registered Venezuelan company or equivalent capital contribution.
visa_types.notes: Granted for 3 years (multiple entry, no stay limit per trip), extendable up to 2 additional years. After this, can apply for Resident status (5-year validity). No minimum investment amount specified on official consular pages. Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Rentista (TR-RE)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who can demonstrate a stable passive income (rentier income) allowing them to support themselves in Venezuela without employment.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources. Suitable for retirees or others with independent income sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Rentista (TR-RE) - Retirement
visa_types.purpose: The TR-RE (Rentista) visa category also covers retired foreign nationals who can demonstrate sufficient passive/pension income to reside in Venezuela without working.
visa_types.notes: Venezuela does not have a dedicated retirement visa; retirees typically apply under the TR-RE (Rentista) category. Exact income thresholds, requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources; contact the nearest Venezuelan embassy or consulate.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Familiar Venezolano (TR-FV)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who are family members of a Venezuelan citizen and wish to reside in or visit Venezuela based on that family relationship.
visa_types.notes: Applies to direct family members (spouse, children, parents) of Venezuelan nationals. Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements and fees not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Familiar Migrante Permanente (TR-FMP)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who are family members of a permanent resident (migrante permanente) in Venezuela, allowing them to join the resident family member.
visa_types.notes: Applies to family members of Venezuelan permanent residents (not citizens). Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements and fees not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Tránsito (V-T)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through Venezuelan territory or airports en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Required for transit passengers who need to pass through Venezuelan immigration. Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact stay limit for transit, fees, and other requirements not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Religioso (TR-REL)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Venezuela to carry out religious activities, missionary work, or ministry as part of a recognized religious organization.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Requires documentation from a recognized religious institution. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Empresario/Industrial (TR-EI)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign entrepreneurs or industrial operators who wish to establish or operate a business or industrial enterprise in Venezuela.
visa_types.notes: Distinct from the TR-I Investor visa. Intended for entrepreneurs and industrial operators. Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Empleado Doméstico (TR-ED)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals employed as domestic workers in the household of a Venezuelan citizen or legal resident.
visa_types.notes: Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Requires proof of domestic employment and sponsor details. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources.

visa_types.name: Visa de Transeúnte Reingreso (TR-RI)
visa_types.purpose: A re-entry visa for foreign nationals who held a previous Venezuelan visa or residence status and wish to return to Venezuela.
visa_types.notes: Re-entry visa for former residents or visa holders returning to Venezuela. Must be applied for at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate. Exact requirements, fees, and validity not published on official online sources.
